include: One seasonal fruit daily (Amla, Guava, or Papaya)
A small bowl of fresh Dahi (Curd) daily
Include one serving of Dal or Sprouts per day
avoid: Tea or Coffee on an empty stomach
diet.meal_timing: Follow a consistent meal schedule. Eat breakfast within 1 hour of waking up.

weekly_schedule.activity: 15–20 min of any activity you enjoy — Walking, Dancing, or light Yoga
Malasana (Deep Squat pose) — hold for 1 minute to maintain pelvic health

exercise.tip: Consistency matters more than intensity. Even a 15-minute walk every day is enough at this stage.

tips: Wake up and sleep at roughly the same time every day to keep your Pulse Rate and Cortisol stable.
Sit in morning sunlight for 10–15 minutes (balcony or near a window) to naturally support Vitamin D3.
Avoid screens 30 minutes before bed for better sleep quality.
lifestyle.doctor_advice: Your risk is currently low. Keep tracking your cycle monthly and schedule an annual check-up.

include: Switch from Maida to Atta (Whole Wheat) or Jowar flour
Start your morning with Methi (Fenugreek) seed water — soak 1 tsp overnight
Ensure at least 50% of your plate is Sabzi (Ghiya, Tori, Bhindi, or Palak)
Add Pudina (Spearmint) chutney or tea to your daily routine
Include a bowl of Dal, Chana, or Sprouts in every main meal
Use Sarson (Mustard) oil or small amounts of Ghee instead of refined oil
avoid: Maida-based items (Biscuits, White Bread, Samosa, Bhatura)
Limit Sugar to once daily — including in tea and desserts
Limit Gur (Jaggery) — it spikes blood sugar almost as fast as white sugar
diet.meal_timing: Eat 3 balanced meals. Never skip breakfast. Aim to finish dinner before 9 PM.

weekly_schedule.activity: 10-minute Post-Meal Walk — use the food's sugar before it stores as fat
Chakki Chalan (Churning Mill pose) — sit and rotate torso, excellent for Cycle Regularity

exercise.tip: The Post-Meal Walk is the single most important habit for managing blood sugar and weight at this risk level.

tips: Finish dinner by 8:30 PM and aim to sleep by 10:30 PM. Late nights are a primary driver of high LH/FSH ratios.
Get 15 minutes of direct morning sunlight to naturally manage your Vitamin D3 levels.
Never have Tea or Coffee with your meals — it blocks iron absorption and affects Hemoglobin (Hb).
Have your morning chai only after eating a small snack (nuts, makhana) to prevent cortisol spikes.
Start tracking your menstrual cycle dates on any app or a simple notebook.
lifestyle.doctor_advice: We strongly recommend consulting a gynecologist for a hormonal blood panel (LH, FSH, AMH, and TSH).

include: The Plate Rule: 50% Sabzi, 25% Dal/Protein, 25% Roti/Rice — at every meal
Methi-Ajwain water first thing every morning (soak overnight, drink on waking)
Sprouts or Chana at lunch daily for protein and fiber
Replace all cooking oil with Sarson (Mustard) Oil or pure Ghee (in small quantity)
Add Pudina water or fresh Pudina chutney daily to help lower androgen levels
Include Curd (Dahi) instead of Buffalo Milk to reduce androgen-linked inflammation
avoid: STRICT: Complete break from Maida for at least 4 weeks
STRICT: No White Sugar — switch to a small amount of Stevia or completely eliminate
Avoid Gur (Jaggery) entirely if RBS is elevated
Avoid heavy Buffalo Milk, especially if Acne or Hair Growth is reported
diet.meal_timing: Eat dinner strictly before 8 PM. A 12-hour gap between dinner and breakfast (e.g., 8 PM to 8 AM) is highly beneficial for insulin regulation.

weekly_schedule.activity: Chair Squats (10 reps x 3 sets) + Wall Push-ups — builds muscle for insulin sensitivity
Mandatory 10-minute Post-Meal Walk after both Lunch and Dinner
10 minutes of Anulom Vilom (Alternate Nostril Breathing) to lower high Pulse Rate and Cortisol
5 rounds of Surya Namaskar as a full-body reset

exercise.tip: Focus on Muscle Loading (Squats, Push-ups) over pure Cardio at this stage — muscles are the body's primary insulin disposal system.

tips: CRITICAL: Lights out strictly by 10:30 PM. No screens at least 1 hour before bed. Sleep deprivation directly spikes LH and Cortisol.
Track your Weight and Waist-Hip Ratio every week — note it down to see real progress.
Spend 15 minutes in morning sunlight daily. This is the easiest way to address low Vitamin D3 without supplements.
Practice the 'Chai Rule': Never drink Tea or Coffee on an empty stomach or with meals. This protects your Hemoglobin (Hb).
Consider a daily stress-relief habit: 10 minutes of journaling, prayer, or simply sitting quietly away from your phone.
lifestyle.doctor_advice: ⚠️ Your risk is significantly elevated. Please consult a gynecologist or endocrinologist as soon as possible for a full hormonal panel.

diet_add: Add Ajwain (Carom Seeds) to your Tadka daily — helps with bloating and fat metabolism
diet_avoid: Avoid eating rice at dinner — switch to a small Roti or soup instead
exercise_add: Prioritize Chair Squats and Resistance exercises over pure walking for better insulin sensitivity
lifestyle_add: Weigh yourself weekly at the same time (morning, before eating) to track progress accurately

diet_add: Replace Buffalo Milk with Cow Milk or Curd (Dahi)
Add fresh Pudina (Spearmint) water or chutney daily — clinically shown to lower androgens
diet_avoid: Avoid heavy dairy (Paneer, Butter) — high saturated fat increases androgen activity
lifestyle_add: Use non-comedogenic or oil-free skincare products
Change your pillowcase every 2–3 days to reduce bacteria

diet_add: Soak and eat 5–7 Badam (Almonds) daily for scalp nourishment
Add Amla (Indian Gooseberry) — fresh, juice, or powder — for DHT reduction
lifestyle_add: Avoid tight hairstyles that stress the scalp
Apply Bhringraj or Coconut oil with a gentle scalp massage twice a week

high_rbs.description: Triggered when RBS > 140 mg/dL (elevated blood sugar)
diet_add: Methi (Fenugreek) seed water is mandatory every morning
Cinnamon (Dalchini) — half teaspoon in warm water daily — helps regulate blood sugar
diet_avoid: Strict avoidance of Gur (Jaggery), Honey, and all sweets
No fruit juices — eat whole fruits only, with their fiber
lifestyle_add: Walk for exactly 10 minutes after every single meal — this is non-negotiable for blood sugar management

exercise_add: Chakki Chalan (Churning Mill exercise) for 10 minutes — the best traditional exercise for cycle regularity
Malasana (Deep Squat) — hold for 1 minute daily to improve pelvic blood flow
lifestyle_add: Try to maintain a consistent sleep-wake schedule 7 days a week — even on weekends
Track your cycle start dates in a notebook or app — even irregular cycles have their own pattern
Spend 15 minutes in direct morning sunlight (before 10 AM) daily — expose arms and legs if possible
Hang your laundry outside or water plants to naturally build sun time into your routine

fertility_focus.description: Triggered when marriage_years > 5 and risk is Moderate or High
lifestyle_add: Track your ovulation window (typically day 10–18 of your cycle) using a Basal Body Temperature thermometer
Consistency in sleeping by 10 PM is vital — it directly regulates the LH surge responsible for ovulation
Reduce any high-intensity physical stress during the ovulation window
diet_add: Increase Folate-rich foods: Palak (Spinach), Methi, and Sprouts are excellent natural sources

sedentary_no_exercise.description: Triggered when exercise == 0 (No regular exercise reported)
exercise_add: Start with just the 10-minute Post-Meal Walk — this one habit alone significantly improves insulin sensitivity
Chakki Chalan can be done while watching TV — no special equipment or space needed

high_fast_food.description: Triggered when fast_food == 1 (High fast food intake)
diet_add: Prepare a batch of Roasted Chana or Makhana at the start of the week as a replacement snack
Keep cut fruits or a bowl of Dahi ready in the fridge to avoid reaching for packaged snacks
diet_avoid: Avoid ordering food after 7 PM — cook simple, quick meals instead (e.g., Khichdi, Dalia, Egg)

lifestyle_add: High stress means high Cortisol. Focus heavily on stress management rather than high-intensity workouts
Add 10 minutes of Anulom Vilom or deep breathing daily

exercise_add: Start extremely small: Commit to just a 10-minute walk after lunch. Do not attempt heavy workouts yet to avoid burnout.
Begin with light mobility or stretching for 5 minutes a day before building up to structured workouts.

lifestyle_add: Since you exercise daily, ensure you are not over-training, which can elevate cortisol and mimic PCOS symptoms. Prioritize at least one active recovery day.
Your water intake is low. Dehydration directly worsens insulin resistance. Drink at least 8 glasses of water daily.
Since you have a desk job, set an alarm to stand up and stretch for 2 minutes every hour to improve blood circulation.
exercise_add: Incorporate desk stretches, neck rotations, and short walks during your office breaks to combat sedentarism.

diet_add: As an athlete, ensure you are getting adequate complex carbohydrates and high-quality protein to support recovery and hormone balance.
lifestyle_add: Monitor your training load carefully. Over-training can spike cortisol and worsen hormonal imbalances like PCOS.
Limit late-night studying and prioritize 7-8 hours of sleep. Screen light from laptops disrupts melatonin and exacerbates hormonal issues.
diet_add: Keep healthy snacks like roasted makhana or nuts on your study desk to avoid reaching for sugary/junk food during long study sessions.

lifestyle_add: Remember to prioritize yourself. Carve out 30 minutes of uninterrupted 'me-time' daily for your meals and exercise without distractions.
exercise_add: While household chores keep you active, they don't replace structured exercise. Dedicate 20 minutes to yoga or a brisk walk specifically for fitness.
